narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings, especially after all the effort you've put into your journey toward law school, and I want to walk you through why our prediction model saw your path to passing the bar exam as a coin flip—about a 51% chance of not making it—leading to this tough call on your application. Picture the model piecing together your story like chapters in a book: the strongest chapter was your Year 3 decile of 1, placing you at the very bottom of your school's rankings that year, which painted the clearest picture of struggle ahead on the bar. Right behind that was your LSAT score of 14.5, which signaled to the model a foundational hurdle in the kind of analytical firepower needed for bar success. Your Year 1 decile of 2 added another early layer of concern, showing this wasn't a one-off dip but a pattern starting from the beginning. Even your middle family income bracket played a role, as it didn't offer the extra safety net that sometimes helps others push through. Your Asian background, while a source of strength for many, tilted the model's view toward added pressure in this high-stakes arena. Your solid undergraduate GPA of 3.2 tried to balance things but couldn't outweigh those heavier pulls, and being a full-time female student rounded out a profile where every piece nudged toward that riskier outcome. It's like the model saw a determined young woman committing fully to law, but with these interconnected hurdles stacking up against the odds students typically beat to pass the bar. This isn't about your potential or heart—it's the model connecting these dots from data on thousands like you. The good news? You're targeting law school with clear intent, and focusing on boosting those deciles or LSAT-like skills could rewrite your story next time. You've got this—keep pushing.
